You will have regular clinic visits for blood tests, blood pressure checks, and heart monitoring. The study team will track your health throughout the trial to watch for any changes or side effects.

How often do I need to visit the clinic?

The exact schedule of visits will be explained to you by the study team, but you should expect regular appointments throughout the trial period. Visits typically include blood work and heart-related evaluations to monitor your health. The study team will work with you to schedule visits at convenient times.

What are the possible risks of joining?

Like all medications, BNV-CRD01 may cause side effects, though we do not yet know all of them since this medicine is still being tested. Common side effects from similar heart medicines can include headache, dizziness, or stomach upset, though not everyone experiences these. The study team will monitor you closely and will stop your participation immediately if they see any serious safety concerns.

What are the potential benefits?

BNV-CRD01 is being tested because early research suggests it may help reduce the risk of heart problems in people with coronary artery disease. However, there is no guarantee that this medicine will help you personally, since the study is still determining if it works. Some participants may receive a placebo instead of the active medication.

Will the study cost me money?

The study sponsor covers the cost of the study drug and most study-related visits and tests. You may have out-of-pocket costs related to your regular medical care that are not directly part of the study, so you should ask the study team about what costs you might be responsible for. Travel expenses to clinic visits are typically your responsibility unless the study team discusses other arrangements with you.

What happens if I experience a serious side effect?

The study team monitors all participants closely for safety, and they have trained staff and emergency procedures in place to help you immediately. You should report any concerning symptoms to the study team right away, and you can withdraw from the study at any time without penalty. The study includes insurance and medical care coverage for study-related injuries.

How long is the study?

The exact duration will be provided by the study team during enrollment, but Phase 3 trials typically last several months to over a year depending on the research questions being answered. You will be told the expected time commitment before you decide to participate. The study team will keep you informed about the timeline throughout your involvement.

Can I continue my regular heart medications?

Yes, you will continue taking your regular blood pressure and heart medications during the study. The study drug (BNV-CRD01 or placebo) is added to your existing treatment plan, not as a replacement. Your study doctor will review all your current medications to make sure they work safely with the study drug.

How do I enroll in CARDIO-301?

Talk to your primary care doctor or cardiologist about the study, or you can visit BioNova Therapeutics' website or call their patient enrollment line to learn more about locations near you. A study team member will review your medical records and discuss whether you meet the eligibility requirements. If you are a good fit, you will have an in-person screening visit to confirm your eligibility before you officially join.

Can I withdraw from the study at any time?

Yes, you can withdraw from the study at any time without any penalty and without affecting your regular medical care. If you decide to leave, simply let the study team know, and they will make sure your transition back to your regular doctors is smooth. Your decision to participate is entirely voluntary.
clinical_trial_faq_copd-inhaler#52dea806:
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS ABOUT BREATHE-101

WHAT IS BREATHE-101?

BREATHE-101 is a research study testing a new medicine called BNV-CPD01 in people with COPD, which is a long-term lung disease that makes breathing difficult. The study will help doctors understand if this medicine is safe and how well it works to help people with COPD breathe better.

WHO CAN JOIN THIS STUDY?

You may be eligible if you are between 40 and 80 years old, have been diagnosed with COPD, have had at least one flare-up of your symptoms in the past year, and have a history of smoking with at least 10 pack-years (for example, smoking one pack per day for 10 years). Your lung function will also need to be in a certain range that your doctor can check with a breathing test.

WHO CANNOT JOIN THIS STUDY?

You would not be eligible if you have active asthma, lung cancer, need more than 4 liters of oxygen per minute, have had a lung infection in the past 4 weeks, or have had lung transplant or lung reduction surgery. It is important to tell the study team about all your medical history so they can determine if the study is right for you.

WHAT WILL HAPPEN IF I JOIN?

If you join BREATHE-101, you will receive either a low dose of BNV-CPD01, a high dose of BNV-CPD01, or a placebo, which is a dummy medicine that looks like the real medicine but has no active ingredient. You will be randomly assigned to one of these three groups, meaning it will be decided by chance, similar to a coin flip.

WHAT IS A PLACEBO AND WHY IS IT USED?

A placebo is an inactive substance that looks and tastes like the real medicine but contains no active drug. Placebos help researchers understand if improvements in symptoms come from the actual medicine or from other factors like extra attention from doctors.

HOW MUCH TIME WILL THE STUDY TAKE?

The exact schedule will be explained when you meet with the study team, but Phase 1 studies typically involve several visits to the research site for check-ups, breathing tests, and blood work. You will be given a full list of visit dates and how long each visit takes before you decide to join.

WILL THE STUDY COST ME MONEY?

No, the study medicine and study-related visits are provided at no cost to you. However, you are responsible for any transportation costs to get to the study site and any costs related to medical care outside of the study.

WHAT ARE THE RISKS OF JOINING?

Like all medicines, BNV-CPD01 may cause side effects, though we do not yet know exactly what they will be because this is the first study in humans. The study team will carefully watch for any problems and explain the known risks before you join.

WHAT BENEFITS MIGHT I RECEIVE?

We hope that the study medicine may help improve your breathing, but there is no guarantee that it will help you personally. Even if the medicine does not help you, the information doctors learn from your participation may help future patients with COPD.

HOW DO I ENROLL IN BREATHE-101?

To learn more about the study and see if you qualify, contact BioNova Global Headquarters or visit the study information website, where you can fill out a brief questionnaire. A member of the study team will then reach out to you to discuss whether BREATHE-101 is right for you.

WHAT IF I WANT TO STOP PARTICIPATING?

You can stop participating in the study at any time for any reason, and you do not have to explain why. Leaving the study will not affect your regular medical care or your relationship with your doctor.

CAN I TAKE MY OTHER MEDICATIONS WHILE IN THE STUDY?

You should continue taking your regular COPD medications unless the study doctor tells you to stop. Tell the study team about all medications and supplements you are taking so they can make sure they do not interfere with the study medicine.
clinical_trial_faq_copd-inhaler#56256b17:
BREATHE-101 Clinical Trial: Frequently Asked Questions

What is the BREATHE-101 trial?

BREATHE-101 is a research study testing a new medicine called BNV-CPD01 to help people with COPD, a long-term lung disease that makes breathing difficult. BioNova Therapeutics is running this study to learn if this medicine is safe and if it helps people breathe better.

Who can join this trial?

You may be able to join if you are between 40 and 80 years old and have been diagnosed with COPD. You also need to be a current or former smoker with at least a 10 pack-year history (for example, smoking one pack per day for 10 years, or half a pack per day for 20 years), and you must have had at least one flare-up of your COPD symptoms in the past year.

What lung tests do I need to have before joining?

Your lung function will be tested using a simple breathing test called spirometry, which measures how much air your lungs can hold and how quickly you can breathe it out. Your results must fall within a specific range that the study doctors use to determine if you're eligible.

Will this trial work if I use supplemental oxygen at home?

You can join if you use supplemental oxygen, but only if you use 4 liters per minute or less. If you need more oxygen than that, you would not be able to participate in this study.

Can I join if I have asthma or a history of lung cancer?

No, people with active asthma or a history of lung cancer cannot participate in this trial. If you have had lung transplant surgery or lung volume reduction surgery in the past, you also would not be able to join.

What happens during the trial visits?

During your visits to the clinic, the study doctors will perform breathing tests, check your vital signs like blood pressure and heart rate, and ask you about how you are feeling. You may also have blood tests or other routine health checks to make sure the medicine is safe for you.

How long will I be in the trial?

Since this is a Phase 1 trial, which focuses on safety in a small group of people, the exact length will be explained to you during the screening process. The study team will provide you with a detailed timeline of all visits and procedures before you decide to join.

What medicine will I receive?

You will receive either the new medicine BNV-CPD01 at a low dose, BNV-CPD01 at a high dose, or a placebo, which is a dummy medicine with no active ingredients. You will not know which one you get, and the study doctors will carefully watch how you respond to it.

Are there any risks to joining this trial?

All medicines carry some risk of side effects, and since BNV-CPD01 is new, some side effects may not be fully known yet. The study doctors will watch you closely and explain all possible risks before you join so you can make an informed decision.

Do I have to pay to be in this trial?

No, you will not have to pay for the study medicine or the visits related to the trial. However, ask the study team about whether there are any costs for travel or other expenses.

How do I know if I'm eligible before committing to the trial?

The study team will do a screening process where they review your medical history, do breathing tests, and ask questions about your lung health. This screening helps them determine if you meet all the requirements without any obligation for you to continue if you decide it's not right for you.

Can I stop participating whenever I want?

Yes, you can leave the trial at any time for any reason without penalty. If you choose to stop, the study team will make sure you receive proper care and that your leaving is documented safely.

How do I enroll in BREATHE-101?

The trial is not yet recruiting participants, but you can contact BioNova Therapeutics or visit the study information provided to you to learn when enrollment will begin. When the trial opens, the study team will guide you through the screening and enrollment process step by step.
clinical_trial_faq_copd_inhaler#52dea806:
BREATHE-101 Trial: Frequently Asked Questions

What is the BREATHE-101 trial?

BREATHE-101 is a research study testing a new medication called BNV-CPD01 to help people with COPD, a lung disease that makes breathing difficult. This is an early-stage study (called Phase 1) where we are primarily checking if the medication is safe and well-tolerated.

Who can participate in this trial?

You may be eligible if you are between 40 and 80 years old, have been diagnosed with COPD, have had at least one flare-up (called an exacerbation) of your symptoms in the past year, and have a significant smoking history of at least 10 pack-years (meaning 10 cigarettes a day for 10 years, or equivalent). We will also check your lung function to make sure it falls within the study range.

Who cannot participate in this trial?

You would not be eligible if you have active asthma, lung cancer, or a respiratory infection within the past 4 weeks. You also cannot participate if you use supplemental oxygen above 4 liters per minute, or if you have had a lung transplant or lung volume reduction surgery in the past.

What does the study involve?

The study will involve visits to our research clinic where you will receive either a low dose of BNV-CPD01, a high dose of BNV-CPD01, or a placebo (inactive treatment that looks like the real medication but has no active ingredient). We will monitor your health closely with breathing tests, blood work, and other assessments.

How long will I be in the study?

The exact duration will be discussed with the research team once you enroll, but Phase 1 trials typically involve several weeks to a few months of active participation plus follow-up visits. We will provide you with a detailed schedule during the informed consent discussion.

What is a placebo and why is it used?

A placebo is a treatment that looks identical to the real medication but contains no active ingredient. Placebos help us understand what improvement happens naturally versus what happens because of the actual medication. About one-third of study participants will receive a placebo.

Will the study cost me anything?

Study visits and all trial-related medical care and testing will be provided at no cost to you. We will also discuss compensation for your time and travel expenses. Please ask the research team about specific details regarding payment.

What are the possible risks of participating?

Like all medications, BNV-CPD01 may cause side effects, though we do not yet know what these might be since this is an early-stage study. Possible risks include mild to moderate reactions at the injection or application site, temporary changes in how you feel, or other effects we will monitor closely. The research team will explain all known risks before you decide whether to join.

What are the possible benefits?

We hope that BNV-CPD01 may help improve your breathing and reduce COPD flare-ups, though we cannot guarantee this in a Phase 1 study. Even if you do not experience direct medical benefit, your participation will help us develop better treatments for people with COPD.

How is my safety monitored during the trial?

Your safety is our top priority. We will perform regular breathing tests, blood work, and check your vital signs at each visit. You will have direct contact with the research team, and you can call us anytime if you have concerns or experience new symptoms between visits.

Can I stop participating if I change my mind?

Yes, you can withdraw from the study at any time without penalty and without affecting your regular medical care. If you decide to leave, we will discuss any necessary follow-up care to keep you safe.

How do I enroll or learn more?

To express interest or ask more questions, please contact BioNova Therapeutics directly through our website or call our patient recruitment team. A member of our research staff will discuss eligibility, answer your questions, and guide you through the enrollment process if you choose to participate.
clinical_trial_faq_diabetes-prevention#6:
DIABPREV-201 TRIAL: FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS

ABOUT THE TRIAL

What is the DIABPREV-201 trial?

DIABPREV-201 is a research study run by BioNova Therapeutics to test an experimental medicine called BNV-DX01 in people with type 2 diabetes. The study will help us understand if this medicine can help manage blood sugar levels better. We are looking for about 300 people to participate.

What is the purpose of this study?

This study is designed to see if BNV-DX01 is safe and if it works to help control blood sugar in adults with type 2 diabetes. By joining, you'll help doctors learn whether this new treatment might help people with diabetes in the future.

How long has this trial been running?

DIABPREV-201 is currently enrolling participants and is in Phase 2, which is an earlier stage of testing. We are not currently recruiting new participants at this time, but if you are interested, you can contact BioNova Therapeutics to learn about future opportunities.

ELIGIBILITY AND PARTICIPATION

Am I eligible to join this trial?

You may be eligible if you are between 30 and 70 years old, have type 2 diabetes, are generally in good health, and have been taking a stable dose of metformin (a common diabetes medicine) for at least 3 months. Your blood sugar control level (measured by HbA1c, a test showing your average blood sugar over 3 months) should be between 7.0% and 10.5%, and your body mass index should be between 25 and 40.

What conditions would prevent me from joining?

You cannot participate if you have type 1 diabetes or gestational diabetes (diabetes during pregnancy), if your kidneys are not working well enough (measured by a test called eGFR), or if you have a history of diabetic ketoacidosis (a serious blood sugar emergency). You also cannot join if you've used insulin within the past 3 months.

What does my participation involve?

As a participant, you will take either the study medicine at one of two doses or a placebo (a pill with no active medicine) as assigned by the research team. You'll attend regular study visits for check-ups, blood tests, and to report how you're feeling.

WHAT TO EXPECT

What are the possible risks of joining this trial?

Like all medicines being tested, BNV-DX01 may cause side effects that we don't yet fully understand because it's still being studied. The research team will monitor you closely during the trial and let you know about any risks that become known. You can stop participating at any time if you're uncomfortable.

What are the potential benefits?

You may benefit from closer medical monitoring and more frequent check-ups than you would normally receive. If BNV-DX01 works, it might help control your blood sugar better, though there is no guarantee that you will personally benefit.

TIME AND COST COMMITMENT

How much time will the trial take?

The exact schedule depends on how long the trial runs, but you can expect to attend multiple clinic visits over several weeks or months. The research team will give you a detailed schedule during your screening visit so you can decide if you can commit the time needed.

Will I have to pay to participate?

No, you will not have to pay to be in this study. The trial sponsor, BioNova Therapeutics, covers all study-related costs, including the study medicine and most visits. You may receive compensation for your time and travel, but the research team will explain this during your screening.

GETTING MORE INFORMATION

How do I enroll or learn more?

To express interest or get more information about DIABPREV-201, contact BioNova Therapeutics directly through their main website or call their research coordinator. They can answer your specific questions and determine if you meet the eligibility requirements. Remember, we are not currently recruiting new participants, but you can ask about future studies or trials.
clinical_trial_faq_diabetes-prevention#9:
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS ABOUT DIABPREV-201

ABOUT THE TRIAL

What is DIABPREV-201?
DIABPREV-201 is a research study testing a new medicine called BNV-DX01 to see if it can help people with type 2 diabetes manage their blood sugar better. The study is being conducted by BioNova Therapeutics and is currently enrolling participants.

What is the purpose of this study?
This study is designed to learn whether BNV-DX01 is safe and effective for people with type 2 diabetes who are already taking metformin. Researchers want to see if this new medicine can help lower blood sugar levels and improve overall health.

ELIGIBILITY AND REQUIREMENTS

Am I eligible to join this trial?
You may be eligible if you are between 30 and 70 years old, have type 2 diabetes, take a stable dose of metformin for at least 3 months, have a BMI (body mass index) between 25 and 40, and have good enough kidney function. You also cannot have type 1 diabetes, gestational diabetes, a history of diabetic ketoacidosis (a serious condition where blood becomes too acidic), or have used insulin in the past 3 months.

What does ECOG 1 or better mean?
ECOG is a scale doctors use to measure how well you can do everyday activities. ECOG 1 means you can do most normal activities, though you may spend some time resting during the day due to your condition.

What is HbA1c and why does it matter for this study?
HbA1c is a blood test that measures your average blood sugar control over the past 2 to 3 months. For this study, your HbA1c must be between 7.0% and 10.5%, which helps researchers include people whose diabetes is not completely controlled but not severely out of range.

WHAT TO EXPECT

What will happen during the study?
You will be randomly assigned to receive either BNV-DX01 (at one of two doses) or a placebo (a dummy pill with no active medicine), and you will take your assigned treatment along with your regular metformin. You will have regular visits to the study site for blood tests, health checks, and to discuss how you are feeling.

How long will I be in the study?
The exact duration will be explained when you enroll, but you should expect multiple clinic visits over several weeks or months. The study team will give you a detailed schedule when you join.

TIME COMMITMENT AND COSTS

How much time will the study take?
You will need to attend regular appointments at the study site for blood work, physical exams, and check-ins with the study doctor. Most people can fit these visits into their regular schedule, though the exact time commitment will be reviewed with you before you enroll.

Will I have to pay for the study medicine?
No, the study medicine will be provided to you at no cost by BioNova Therapeutics. You will not be charged for any study-related visits, blood tests, or medicines given as part of the research.

SAFETY AND RISKS

What are the possible risks or side effects?
Like all medicines, BNV-DX01 may cause side effects, which could range from mild to serious. The study team will monitor you closely and explain all known risks before you join, and you can ask questions about any concerns.

What if I have a bad reaction to the medicine?
The study team will be available to help you throughout the trial, and you should contact them immediately if you experience any concerning symptoms. You also have the right to stop taking the medicine and leave the study at any time without penalty.

HOW TO ENROLL

How do I sign up for DIABPREV-201?
Contact BioNova Global Headquarters or ask your doctor for information about study sites near you. The research team will answer your questions, check if you qualify, and walk you through the consent process, where you will learn all the details before agreeing to participate.

What does informed consent mean?
Informed consent means that the study team will explain everything about the trial in plain language, answer all your questions, and give you time to decide whether you want to participate before you sign any paperwork. You are never obligated to join, and you can change your mind at any time.
clinical_trial_faq_diabetes_prevention#9:
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS ABOUT DIABPREV-201

What is the DIABPREV-201 study?

DIABPREV-201 is a research study testing whether a new medicine called BNV-DX01 can help people with type 2 diabetes manage their condition better. This is a phase 2 study, which means we are still learning if the medicine is safe and works the way we hope it will.

Who can join this study?

You may be able to join if you are between 30 and 70 years old, have type 2 diabetes, are generally in good health, and have been taking a stable dose of metformin for at least three months. Your diabetes control level (measured by a blood test called HbA1c) should be between 7.0% and 10.5%, and your weight should fall within certain ranges for your height.

What medical conditions would keep me from joining?

You cannot join if you have type 1 diabetes or gestational diabetes, or if you have had severe kidney problems (eGFR below 45). You also cannot participate if you have a history of diabetic ketoacidosis, which is a serious diabetes emergency, or if you have used insulin within the past three months.

What does the study involve?

You will receive either the test medicine BNV-DX01 at one of two doses, or a placebo (a pill with no medicine), and we will track how it affects your diabetes over time. You will have regular visits to the study center for blood tests, blood pressure checks, and other routine health measurements.

How long will I be in the study?

The exact length depends on the study design, but you should expect multiple visits over several months. We will tell you the full schedule before you decide to join so you can plan accordingly.

What is the time commitment for each visit?

Most visits take between one and two hours, including time for check-in, talking with the study team, blood work, and other health measurements. Some visits may be shorter and some longer, depending on what tests are being done that day.

Will the study cost me money?

There is no cost to you for the study medicine or the medical visits required by the research. However, you may need to cover your own travel to the study center unless otherwise arranged.

What are the possible risks?

Like all medicines, BNV-DX01 may cause side effects, though we do not yet know how common they are or how serious they might be. We will monitor you closely throughout the study and tell you right away if we find any safety concerns.

How will my privacy be protected?

All information you share will be kept private and secure, and your personal identity will not be used in any study reports or publications. Only the study team and authorized people will have access to your medical information.

How do I enroll or get more information?

You can contact BioNova Global Headquarters through your healthcare provider, or ask about the study at your diabetes clinic. The study team will answer your questions and help you decide if DIABPREV-201 is right for you.

Can I stop participating whenever I want?

Yes, you can leave the study at any time for any reason, and doing so will not affect your regular medical care. We just ask that you let the study team know so they can follow up on your health.

What happens after the study ends?

The study team will discuss the results with you and explain what they mean for your diabetes care. Depending on the findings, your regular doctor may talk with you about next steps for your treatment.
clinical_trial_faq_diabex_p2#06823e5f: DIABEX-201 is a Phase 2 clinical trial studying a dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist for type 2 diabetes with comorbid hypertension. The trial compares two dose levels of the investigational drug against placebo. To be eligible, participants must be between 30 and 70 years of age with confirmed type 2 diabetes, HbA1c between 7.0% and 10.5%, BMI between 25 and 40, and stable metformin use for at least three months. Participants with type 1 diabetes, gestational diabetes, eGFR below 45, or recent diabetic ketoacidosis are not eligible. This trial has completed enrollment.
clinical_trial_faq_her2-combo#83e48aec:
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS ABOUT THE HER2COMBO-201 CLINICAL TRIAL

What is the HER2COMBO-201 trial?

HER2COMBO-201 is a research study being run by BioNova Therapeutics to test a new combination treatment for HER2-positive breast cancer. We are comparing a new drug called BNV-HER01 combined with trastuzumab (a standard breast cancer medicine) against trastuzumab combined with other standard treatments.

Who can participate in this trial?

You may be eligible if you are between 18 and 75 years old and have been diagnosed with HER2-positive breast cancer that has spread or come back. You will also need to have measurable disease (cancer that can be seen on imaging or scans), adequate heart function, and an overall health status that allows you to tolerate treatment.

What does HER2-positive mean?

HER2-positive means your breast cancer cells have high levels of a protein called HER2 on their surface. This type of cancer often grows faster than other breast cancers, but it also responds well to targeted medicines like trastuzumab that attack the HER2 protein.

What does ECOG performance status mean, and why does it matter?

ECOG performance status measures how well you can carry out daily activities during your illness. An ECOG score of 2 or lower means you can perform light work or self-care but may spend some time in bed or sitting—and this level of health is important because it helps us know if you can safely participate in the study.

What treatments will I receive if I join?

If you are selected for this trial, you will be randomly assigned to receive either BNV-HER01 plus trastuzumab or trastuzumab plus standard treatments. This random assignment helps us fairly compare how well each combination works.

What does the trial schedule look like?

The specific schedule depends on which treatment group you join, but you can expect regular clinic visits for medicine infusions, blood draws, and imaging scans to monitor your cancer and overall health. We will provide you with a detailed calendar of visits and time commitments before you enroll so there are no surprises.

How long will I be in the trial?

The length of participation varies depending on how your cancer responds to treatment and any side effects you experience. We will discuss the expected timeline with you during your screening visit.

Will there be any costs to me as a participant?

There are typically no costs to you for the study drug, study-related tests, or visits that are part of the research. However, you should discuss insurance coverage and any potential costs with the trial coordinator before enrolling.

What are the main risks of participating?

Both treatment options carry side effects, which may include nausea, fatigue, low blood counts, and heart-related concerns. We monitor you closely with regular check-ups and blood tests to catch and manage any problems early.

Why do we check heart function?

Some medicines used to treat HER2-positive breast cancer can affect how well your heart pumps blood, so we test your heart function before you start treatment and regularly during the trial to keep you safe. If your heart function drops below acceptable levels, we may need to adjust your treatment.

What happens if I develop an autoimmune disease during the trial?

Autoimmune disease is a condition where your immune system mistakenly attacks your own body's cells. If you develop this during the trial, we will work with you to manage it, and depending on the severity, it may affect your ability to continue in the study.

What if my cancer has already spread to my brain?

If your cancer has spread to your brain (active CNS metastases), you would not be able to join this particular trial because the study medicines have not been tested in that situation. We encourage you to discuss other treatment options with your doctor.

How do I enroll in the HER2COMBO-201 trial?

To enroll, contact BioNova Therapeutics or ask your oncologist if you are eligible and interested in this study. The study team will schedule a screening visit to confirm your eligibility and answer any remaining questions before you decide to participate.
clinical_trial_faq_her2-combo#d8583aef:
HER2COMBO-201 Clinical Trial: Frequently Asked Questions

What is the HER2COMBO-201 trial?

HER2COMBO-201 is a research study led by BioNova Therapeutics that tests a new combination of medicines for people with HER2-positive breast cancer. We are comparing a new drug called BNV-HER01 combined with the standard medicine trastuzumab against trastuzumab alone to see which works better and is safer. This is a Phase 2 trial, which means we're still learning if this new combination is effective and well-tolerated in patients.

Who can join this trial?

To join HER2COMBO-201, you must be between 18 and 75 years old and have breast cancer that tests positive for HER2, a protein found on some cancer cells. Your cancer must be measurable on imaging scans, and your heart must be working well enough for the study medicines. You will also need to be in good enough health to handle the treatment, which doctors measure using a simple performance scale.

What health conditions might prevent me from joining?

You cannot join if you have an active autoimmune disease, which means a condition where your immune system is actively attacking your own body. You also cannot join if you have received certain anti-cancer drugs called ADCs (antibody-drug conjugates) for metastatic breast cancer in the past, or if you have active cancer in your brain. Additionally, if you have had serious heart problems in the last six months, you will not be eligible.

How much time will I need to commit to this trial?

The exact time commitment will be discussed with the study team, but you can expect regular visits to the research site for treatment, blood tests, and heart monitoring. Visits may be weekly at first, then become less frequent as the trial continues. The study team will work with you to create a schedule that fits your life as much as possible.

What will happen during the trial visits?

During visits, you will receive your study medicine through an IV (a small tube placed in your vein), have blood drawn to check your health, and have your heart function checked with a test called an echocardiogram. Your doctor will also ask you how you are feeling and check for any side effects. Imaging scans, such as CT or MRI, will be done periodically to see how your cancer is responding.

What are the possible side effects?

The study medicines may cause side effects such as tiredness, nausea, low blood cell counts, or heart-related problems, though we hope these will be mild. Because this is a new combination, some side effects may not be fully known yet, and we will monitor you closely throughout the study. Your study doctor will discuss potential side effects with you in detail and help manage any that occur.

What costs will I have to pay?

The study sponsor, BioNova Therapeutics, covers the cost of all study medicines and most tests required for the trial. However, you may have some costs related to regular medical care visits or travel to the study site, which you should discuss with the study team. Many patients also qualify for programs that help cover travel or other trial-related expenses.

How long will I be in the trial?

The length of your time in the study depends on how well the treatment works for you and how your body tolerates it. Most patients stay in the trial for several months to over a year, with ongoing follow-up care afterward. Your study doctor will explain the expected timeline when you meet.

Will my regular doctor be involved?

Yes, your regular doctor will remain involved in your care and will receive updates about your participation in the trial. It is important to keep your regular doctor informed about the study and any side effects you experience. You will also maintain regular appointments outside the trial for routine medical care.

How is my safety monitored in the trial?

A team of doctors and safety experts carefully watches all trial data to make sure participants stay safe. If any serious side effects occur, the study can be paused or stopped immediately to protect you. You will also have regular check-ins with the study team to discuss how you are feeling.

How do I enroll in HER2COMBO-201?

To enroll, contact BioNova Global Headquarters or ask your cancer doctor if they can help you join the trial. The study team will review your medical records to make sure you are eligible and will answer all your questions. If you qualify, you will sign an informed consent form that explains everything about the trial before you begin.

What happens after the trial ends?

After the study ends, your study doctor will discuss what comes next for your cancer treatment with you. Depending on how you respond to the trial medicine, your doctor may recommend continuing treatment or switching to another option. The study team will continue to follow your health for a period of time after the trial to see how the treatment affects you long-term.
clinical_trial_faq_her2_combo#83e48aec:
HER2COMBO-201 Trial: Frequently Asked Questions

What is the HER2COMBO-201 trial studying?

This is a research study testing a new combination treatment for HER2-positive breast cancer, which is a type of breast cancer that grows faster because of a protein called HER2 on cancer cells. The study compares a new drug called BNV-HER01 combined with a standard treatment called trastuzumab against trastuzumab with standard care alone. The goal is to see if adding BNV-HER01 helps patients live longer and have better outcomes.

Who can participate in this trial?

You may be eligible if you are between 18 and 75 years old and have been diagnosed with HER2-positive breast cancer that has been confirmed by a tissue sample (biopsy). You also need to have cancer that can be measured by imaging, a heart function measurement called LVEF above 50%, and a good overall health status. Your doctor can review the full eligibility criteria to confirm whether this trial is right for you.

What health conditions would prevent me from joining?

You would not be able to join if you have an active autoimmune disease (a condition where your body's immune system attacks itself), active cancer that has spread to the brain, or if you have had serious heart problems in the past six months. Also, if you have previously received a type of anti-HER2 drug called an antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) for advanced breast cancer, you would not be eligible. Your doctor will discuss your complete medical history to determine if any of these apply to you.

What does participating involve?

You will receive study treatment either as an outpatient or inpatient depending on the treatment plan, with regular visits to the study clinic for blood tests, imaging scans, and health checks. You will be randomly assigned to receive either BNV-HER01 plus trastuzumab or trastuzumab with standard care. The study team will monitor you closely throughout your participation to watch for both benefits and any side effects.

How long will I be in the trial?

The length of your participation depends on how your cancer responds to treatment and how well you tolerate the study drugs. Most patients continue treatment as long as it is working and they are not experiencing unmanageable side effects. Even after treatment ends, you may have follow-up visits to monitor your health and cancer status.

How much time will I need to commit each month?

Your time commitment will vary but typically includes clinic visits every 1-4 weeks, with some visits lasting several hours for treatment and monitoring. In addition to in-person visits, you may have phone check-ins with the study team between appointments. Your study coordinator can give you a more specific schedule based on which treatment group you are assigned to.

Will I have to pay for the study treatment?

No, the study treatment and most study-related tests and visits are provided at no cost to you as part of the research study. However, you may still be responsible for standard medical care costs not directly related to the trial, such as office visit copays or costs for other medications you take. We recommend discussing any cost concerns with the study coordinator at your site.

What are the possible side effects I should know about?

Like all cancer treatments, BNV-HER01 and trastuzumab can cause side effects, which may include fatigue, nausea, low blood cell counts, and in rare cases, heart problems. The study team will monitor you regularly with blood tests and heart function checks to catch any problems early. You will be told about all known risks before you join, and the team will work with you to manage any side effects that occur.

Is there a guarantee the treatment will work?

No treatment works for everyone, and we cannot promise that this study drug will help your cancer or extend your life. The purpose of this trial is to learn whether BNV-HER01 combined with trastuzumab is more effective than trastuzumab alone. Being in the study gives you access to a new treatment option and close medical monitoring, but there is always a chance your cancer may not respond as hoped.

What happens if I experience a serious side effect?

If you have a serious side effect, contact the study team immediately—they are available to help 24/7 for urgent concerns. The study doctor may pause your treatment, adjust your dose, or switch you to a different treatment to keep you safe. You have the right to withdraw from the study at any time without losing access to standard cancer care.

How do I enroll or find out more information?

You can ask your oncologist (cancer doctor) if this trial might be right for you, or you can visit ClinicalTrials.gov and search for HER2COMBO-201 to find study sites near you. You can also contact BioNova Therapeutics directly for information about trial locations and how to schedule a screening visit. The study team will review your medical records and answer any questions you have before you decide to participate.

What support is available during the trial?

The study team includes nurses, doctors, and coordinators who will answer your questions and help manage any side effects or concerns that come up. Many trial sites offer patient support services, and you can ask about resources like counseling, nutritional support, or financial assistance programs. You are not alone in this process—the entire team is committed to supporting you throughout your participation.
clinical_trial_faq_lungshield_p1#2de9414: LUNGSHIELD-101 is a Phase 1 dose escalation trial studying a bispecific antibody for advanced non-small cell lung cancer. The trial evaluates escalating doses of the investigational agent. To be eligible, participants must be between 18 and 80 years of age with histologically confirmed NSCLC stage IIIB or IV, at least one prior line of therapy, and adequate bone marrow function. Participants with active autoimmune disease, uncontrolled infection, symptomatic brain metastases, or who are pregnant or breastfeeding are not eligible. The trial is recruiting at sites in Houston TX and Tokyo.
clinical_trial_faq_neuregen_p2#dac19d36: NEUREGEN-201 is a Phase 2 clinical trial studying gene therapy for mild-to-moderate Alzheimer disease. The trial compares two dose levels of the gene therapy against placebo. To be eligible, participants must be between 55 and 85 years of age with a clinical diagnosis of mild-to-moderate Alzheimer disease, an MMSE score between 16 and 26, and stable treatment with a cholinesterase inhibitor for at least three months. Participants with active cancer, uncontrolled seizures, or contraindications to lumbar puncture are not eligible. The trial is recruiting at sites in Cambridge MA, Chicago IL, London, and Tokyo.
clinical_trial_faq_oncora-phase1#01cc8f8:
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS ABOUT ONCORA-101

What is ONCORA-101?

ONCORA-101 is a clinical trial sponsored by BioNova Therapeutics that tests a new medicine called BNV-ONC01 in people with advanced lung cancer. This trial has been completed, and the results are being studied by our team to help us understand how well this treatment works and how safe it is.

Who can participate in this trial?

You may be eligible if you are between 18 and 80 years old, have advanced non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC), have received at least one prior cancer treatment, and have adequate bone marrow function. Your doctor will review your complete medical history to determine if the trial is right for you.

Who cannot participate in this trial?

You cannot participate if you have active autoimmune disease (when your immune system attacks your own body), uncontrolled infection, or symptomatic brain metastases (cancer that has spread to the brain and is causing symptoms). You also cannot participate if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, have had prior immunotherapy treatments, or have previously received bispecific antibodies (a type of cancer-fighting protein).

What does "ECOG ≤ 1" mean?

ECOG is a score that describes how well you are able to perform daily activities while dealing with your cancer. An ECOG score of 0 or 1 means you are able to walk around and do most of your normal activities, though you may need to rest more than usual.

What is histologically confirmed NSCLC?

This means a doctor has taken a small tissue sample from your lung and examined it under a microscope to confirm you have non-small cell lung cancer, which is the most common type of lung cancer. This confirmation is important to make sure you have the right type of cancer for this trial.

What will happen during the trial?

You will receive doses of the study medicine BNV-ONC01, and the research team will carefully monitor how your body responds. You will have regular visits for blood tests, imaging scans, and check-ups to track any changes in your cancer and watch for side effects.

How long do I need to participate?

The trial schedule varies depending on your individual response to the treatment and any side effects you may experience. Your doctor will explain the specific time commitment and visit schedule before you enroll.

What are the potential risks of participating?

Like all cancer treatments, BNV-ONC01 may cause side effects, which could range from mild to serious. The research team will monitor you closely and discuss any risks specific to you before you decide to participate.

Will I have to pay for the trial medicine?

No, the study medicine is provided at no cost to you as part of the clinical trial. However, you should ask the research team about coverage for other trial-related visits and tests.

How do I know if I should enroll?

Talk with your cancer doctor about whether ONCORA-101 might be appropriate for you based on your specific cancer stage, prior treatments, and overall health. Your doctor can help you weigh the potential benefits and risks compared to other treatment options available to you.

What happens if I experience severe side effects?

The research team is trained to monitor for and manage side effects, and they can adjust your dose or stop treatment if needed. You can contact the trial team immediately if you experience any concerning symptoms between scheduled visits.

How do I enroll in ONCORA-101?

Please speak with your oncologist (cancer doctor) or contact BioNova Therapeutics directly at BioNova Global Headquarters to ask about trial enrollment and next steps. The research team will answer your questions and help determine if you meet the eligibility requirements.
clinical_trial_faq_oncora-phase1#8ce0e50:
ONCORA-101 Trial: Frequently Asked Questions for Patients and Caregivers

What is the ONCORA-101 trial?

ONCORA-101 is a research study testing a new medicine called BNV-ONC01 in people with advanced lung cancer. The study is being run by BioNova Therapeutics to learn if this treatment is safe and how well it works.

Who can participate in this trial?

You may be able to join if you are 18 to 80 years old, have been diagnosed with advanced lung cancer (a type called non-small cell lung cancer), and have received at least one previous cancer treatment. You will also need to have healthy enough bone marrow and overall body function, which doctors check through blood tests and exams.

Are there reasons I cannot participate?

Yes. You cannot join if you have an active autoimmune disease (when your body's immune system attacks itself), an active infection that is not controlled, or if you have taken certain types of cancer drugs before, including immunotherapy or bispecific antibodies. You also cannot participate if you have untreated cancer in your brain that is causing symptoms, or if you are pregnant or breastfeeding.

What does participating in the trial involve?

Participants receive doses of BNV-ONC01 under close medical supervision and have regular check-ins with the study team. These visits include blood tests, imaging scans, and physical exams to monitor your health and how you are responding to the treatment.

How long does the trial last?

The ONCORA-101 trial has been completed, and 60 patients have already participated. If you are interested in treatment options, your doctor can discuss the results and next steps with you.

What is the time commitment for patients?

Trial visits typically occur frequently at the start of treatment and then become less frequent over time. The exact schedule depends on the study protocol and your individual needs, but you should plan for regular appointments at the research center or hospital over several months.

Will my travel and care costs be covered?

Trial sponsors typically help cover the cost of the study treatment and related medical tests. However, you should ask the study team specifically about what expenses are covered and what you may need to pay out of pocket, including any travel costs.

What are the possible risks of joining this trial?

Like any new medicine, BNV-ONC01 may cause side effects that doctors will watch for carefully during the trial. The study team will monitor you closely and explain any risks specific to this treatment so you can make an informed choice.

What are the possible benefits?

You may benefit from receiving a new treatment option, though there is no guarantee the medicine will help your cancer. You will also receive regular medical care and close monitoring from experienced doctors throughout the trial.

What happens if I experience a serious side effect?

The study team will work quickly to help manage any side effects and will adjust your care as needed. You have the right to stop participating at any time if you feel it is not right for you.

How do I enroll or get more information?

Speak with your oncologist about whether ONCORA-101 or similar clinical trials might be appropriate for you. You can also contact BioNova Therapeutics directly to ask about other trials or treatment options they are testing.

What happens after the trial ends?

The study team will share the results with you and discuss what treatment options are available moving forward based on what was learned. Your doctors will help you decide on the best next steps for your care.
clinical_trial_faq_oncora-phase3#cfe8599:
ONCORA-301 Trial: Frequently Asked Questions

ABOUT THE TRIAL

What is ONCORA-301?

ONCORA-301 is a research study testing a new cancer treatment called a monoclonal antibody (a type of protein that helps your immune system fight cancer) combined with standard care, compared to standard care alone. The study is looking at whether this new treatment helps people with advanced lung cancer live longer and feel better.

Who is running this study?

BioNova Therapeutics is the company sponsoring this research. The study is being conducted at medical centers across the country with doctors and nurses trained to care for cancer patients.

ELIGIBILITY

Am I eligible to join ONCORA-301?

You may be eligible if you are 18 to 75 years old, have been diagnosed with advanced lung cancer (specifically non-small cell lung cancer at stage IIIB or IV), can do light activities around the house, and have not received certain types of immunotherapy in the past 6 months. Your doctor will review your medical history and test results to confirm whether you qualify.

What medical conditions would keep me from joining?

You would not be eligible if you have an active autoimmune disease (a condition where your body's immune system attacks itself), active cancer in your brain, or have had a heart attack or serious heart problem in the past 6 months. Your research team will discuss your full medical history to determine your eligibility.

WHAT TO EXPECT

What happens during the study visits?

You will have regular appointments at the study center for blood tests, imaging scans (like CT scans to check your cancer), heart monitoring, and doctor check-ins. Visit frequency is typically every few weeks, though your schedule may vary based on your individual treatment plan.

Will I receive treatment during the study?

Yes, you will receive either the new monoclonal antibody plus standard cancer treatment, or standard cancer treatment plus a placebo (an inactive substance that looks like medicine but has no active ingredient). You and your doctor will not know which group you are in—this is called randomization and helps researchers get fair results.

How much time will I need to spend at the clinic?

Most visits last 2 to 4 hours, and you may need appointments every 2 to 4 weeks depending on your treatment schedule. Some visits may be shorter if they only involve blood work or check-ins with your nurse.

RISKS AND SIDE EFFECTS

What are the possible risks?

The new monoclonal antibody may cause side effects such as fatigue, fever, rash, or breathing difficulties, similar to other immunotherapy treatments. The standard cancer treatment you receive may also have side effects; your doctor will discuss these with you in detail and monitor you closely throughout the study.

What if I experience side effects?

Tell your research team immediately about any new symptoms or concerns. Your medical team has experience managing side effects and can adjust your treatment plan if needed to help keep you as comfortable as possible.

COSTS AND TIME COMMITMENT

Will I have to pay for the study treatment?

No, the study treatment and most research-related care is provided at no cost to you. However, you may be responsible for routine doctor visits and standard cancer care not related to the research; check with your insurance company and the study team about what is covered.

How long will I be in the study?

The active treatment phase typically lasts several months, and you may be followed for additional check-ups after treatment ends. The total time commitment varies by person, but your research team will give you a clear timeline before you enroll.

HOW TO ENROLL

How do I join ONCORA-301?

Talk with your cancer doctor about whether this study is right for you, or contact BioNova Therapeutics directly to find a study site near you. The research team will schedule an initial appointment to review your medical records, answer your questions, and discuss next steps if you decide to participate.

What happens after I express interest?

A member of the research team will review your medical history and eligibility, then schedule a screening visit where you'll have tests and a detailed conversation with the study doctor. If you are eligible and still interested, you will sign an informed consent form (a legal document explaining the study) before starting treatment.
clinical_trial_faq_oncora-phase3#f59f778:
FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS ABOUT ONCORA-301

What is ONCORA-301?

ONCORA-301 is a clinical trial testing a new medicine called a monoclonal antibody, which is a man-made protein designed to help your immune system fight lung cancer. The trial is being run by BioNova Therapeutics to see if this medicine works better when combined with standard lung cancer treatment.

Who can join this trial?

You may be able to join if you are 18 to 75 years old and have been diagnosed with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (a common type of lung cancer). You will also need to be in reasonably good health overall, with organs like your heart, liver, and kidneys working well enough to handle the trial medicine.

What types of lung cancer qualify?

ONCORA-301 is for people with stage IIIB or IV non-small cell lung cancer that can be measured on imaging scans. Your cancer must be confirmed by a biopsy or tissue sample under a microscope. If you have a different type of lung cancer or an earlier stage, you may not qualify.

What medical conditions would prevent me from joining?

You cannot join if you have an active autoimmune disease, which means your immune system is already attacking your own body. You also cannot participate if you have received certain immunotherapy medicines in the past 6 months, if you have untreated cancer in your brain, or if you have had serious heart problems within the last 6 months.

How many people are in the trial?

About 450 people will join ONCORA-301 across multiple hospitals and cancer centers. As of now, about 287 people have already enrolled.

What will happen during the trial?

Half of the participants will receive the new monoclonal antibody medicine plus standard cancer treatment, and the other half will receive a placebo (a fake medicine that looks like the real one) plus standard treatment. You will need to visit the study site regularly for check-ups, blood tests, and imaging scans to see how the medicine is working.

How much time will the trial take?

The exact schedule depends on how your cancer responds and how long you stay on the treatment, but you should expect regular visits to the hospital or cancer center, likely every few weeks. Your study doctor will explain the specific visit schedule during the consent process before you join.

Does the trial cost me money?

The study medicine and related care for the trial are provided at no cost to you. However, you may be responsible for standard costs like parking, travel, or co-pays for routine care at your center, depending on your insurance.

What are the possible risks?

Like all medicines, the monoclonal antibody could cause side effects, which might include tiredness, fevers, or immune-related reactions where your body attacks itself. Your study team will monitor you closely and can stop or adjust your treatment if serious side effects happen.

What are the potential benefits?

The trial medicine may help slow or shrink your cancer and help you live longer, but this is not guaranteed since the medicine is still being tested. Even if you receive the placebo, you will get standard cancer treatment that your doctor believes is appropriate for your situation.

How will I know if the medicine is working?

Your study doctors will perform regular imaging scans and blood tests to check the size of your cancer and how your body is responding. They will discuss these results with you during your visits.

How do I enroll in ONCORA-301?

Contact BioNova Therapeutics or visit the trial website to find a participating cancer center near you. A study coordinator will review whether you meet the eligibility requirements and explain the process in detail before you decide to join.
clinical_trial_faq_oncora_p3#2664331d: ONCORA-301 is a Phase 3 clinical trial studying a monoclonal antibody treatment for HER2-positive breast cancer. The trial compares the investigational antibody plus standard of care against placebo plus standard of care. To be eligible, participants must be between 18 and 75 years of age with histologically confirmed HER2+ breast cancer, measurable disease per RECIST 1.1 criteria, and adequate organ function. Participants with prior anti-HER2 therapy in the metastatic setting, active CNS metastases, or recent cardiac events are not eligible. The trial is currently recruiting at sites in Cambridge MA, Houston TX, and London.
clinical_trial_faq_oncora_phase1#8ce0e50:
ONCORA-101 Trial: Frequently Asked Questions

What is ONCORA-101?

ONCORA-101 is a research study testing a new medicine called BNV-ONC01 to help treat advanced lung cancer. The study has been completed with 60 patients, and we are sharing information to help you understand what this trial involved and whether it might be right for you or a loved one.

Who can join the ONCORA-101 trial?

You may be eligible if you are between 18 and 80 years old and have been diagnosed with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (a common type of lung cancer that has spread). You must have already received at least one previous lung cancer treatment and have healthy enough bone marrow function, which your doctor can check with a simple blood test.

Are there reasons I might not be able to join this trial?

Yes—you would not be able to join if you have an active autoimmune disease (a condition where your immune system attacks your own body), an uncontrolled infection, or brain tumors causing symptoms. You also cannot join if you are pregnant or breastfeeding, have received immunotherapy before, or have been treated with certain types of cancer drugs called bispecific antibodies.

What does "ECOG ≤ 1" mean in the eligibility requirements?

ECOG is a simple scale doctors use to measure how well you can perform daily activities when you have cancer. An ECOG score of 0 or 1 means you are well enough to be fairly active, though you may need to rest during the day—this helps doctors know if your body can handle the study medicine.

What will happen during the trial visits?

During the trial, you will receive the study medicine and have regular doctor visits to check your health with blood tests, imaging scans, and physical exams. The visits help the research team monitor how the medicine is working and watch for any side effects.

How much time will I need to spend on this trial?

The time commitment varies depending on the phase of the trial and your individual treatment plan, but you should expect multiple clinic visits over several months. Your study doctor will explain the exact schedule before you enroll so you know what to plan for.

Will I have to pay for the study medicine or visits?

Study medicines and most visits related to the trial are typically provided at no cost to you, but you should discuss payment details and any out-of-pocket costs with the trial team before joining. Insurance may still cover some costs, and the research team can help you understand what to expect.

What are the possible risks of joining this trial?

Like all new medicines, BNV-ONC01 may cause side effects, though the exact risks were still being studied in this Phase 1 trial. Common concerns with cancer medicines include fatigue, nausea, and low blood cell counts, but your doctor will monitor you closely and can adjust your treatment if needed.

What are the possible benefits of this trial?

Some patients in the trial may experience slowing or shrinking of their cancer, though there is no guarantee the medicine will help you personally. Even if the medicine does not benefit you directly, the information learned helps doctors develop better treatments for future lung cancer patients.

How do I know if I should enroll in a trial like this?

Talk openly with your cancer doctor about whether a clinical trial is a good fit for your situation, goals, and health. Your doctor can review your medical history and help you weigh the potential benefits and risks based on your specific case.

Can I leave the trial once I start?

Yes—you can stop participating in the trial at any time and for any reason, without affecting your regular cancer care. If you decide to leave, let your study doctor know so they can discuss any important next steps for your treatment.

Where can I learn more or enroll?

Contact BioNova Therapeutics or your cancer care center for more information about available trials and enrollment. You can also speak with your oncologist, who may be able to refer you to trials that match your diagnosis and health status.
clinical_trial_faq_oncora_phase3#f59f778:
ONCORA-301 Clinical Trial: Frequently Asked Questions

What is ONCORA-301?

ONCORA-301 is a research study testing a new cancer medicine combined with standard treatment for advanced lung cancer. The study is being run by BioNova Therapeutics and involves about 450 people across multiple hospitals.

Who can join this trial?

You may be eligible if you are between 18 and 75 years old, have been diagnosed with advanced non-small cell lung cancer (a common type of lung cancer), and have not received certain cancer medicines in the past 6 months. Your doctor can tell you if your health condition meets all the specific requirements needed to participate.

What does non-small cell lung cancer mean?

Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) is one of the two main types of lung cancer. It grows more slowly than small cell lung cancer and is the most common type, accounting for about 85% of all lung cancers.

What medical tests will I need before starting?

Before joining the trial, you will have blood tests and imaging scans to make sure your organs are working well enough to safely participate. These tests help doctors understand your current health and whether the study medicine might be right for you.

Will I definitely get the new medicine?

No—some participants will randomly receive the new medicine combined with standard treatment, while others will receive a placebo (a fake medicine) combined with standard treatment. Neither you nor your doctor will know which group you are in until the study ends.

How often will I visit the hospital?

Your visits will likely be frequent at the start of treatment, with regular blood work and scans to check how you are responding. The exact schedule will be explained to you before you enroll, and the study team will work with you to fit visits into your life.

What are the possible side effects?

Like all medicines, the new treatment may cause side effects, which could range from mild (such as tiredness) to serious (such as heart or immune system problems). You will be told about all known risks before you decide to join, and doctors will watch you closely throughout the study.

Will the trial cost me money?

The study medicine and tests directly related to the trial are typically paid for by BioNova Therapeutics. You should ask the study team about any costs you might have, such as parking or travel to appointments, and whether your insurance will cover standard care you receive as part of the trial.

How long will I be in the study?

The length varies depending on how well the treatment works and your overall health during the trial. The study team will explain the expected timeline and what happens after the study ends.

What happens if I want to leave the trial?

You can leave the study at any time and for any reason—there will be no penalty, and your regular medical care will continue. Let the study team know as soon as possible if you decide to withdraw.

How do I enroll in ONCORA-301?

Talk to your cancer doctor about whether this trial might be right for you, or contact BioNova Therapeutics directly through their website or phone number to find a study location near you. The study team will guide you through the screening process and answer any other questions you have.

Where can I get more information?

You can speak with your doctor, call BioNova Therapeutics, or visit ClinicalTrials.gov to search for ONCORA-301 and find participating hospitals in your area. The study team is available to discuss your eligibility and address any concerns you may have.

enrich_article_clinical#be32034f:
Clinical development at BioNova represents a cornerstone commitment to rigorous scientific investigation and regulatory excellence. Our comprehensive clinical division advances novel therapeutic agents across multiple therapeutic domains, including oncology, immunology, cardiovascular disease, and rare genetic disorders. Each program generates robust efficacy and safety data supporting regulatory submissions and ultimately serving patient populations globally. Under the strategic direction of Castor, our clinical operations maintain alignment with FDA guidance and international regulatory standards through a systematic four-phase approach. Phase I trials establish safety profiles and pharmacokinetic properties in small populations, while Phase II studies evaluate preliminary efficacy in target patient populations enrolling 100 to 300 participants. Phase III trials confirm efficacy and compare investigational products against standard treatments in large randomized controlled populations, followed by Phase IV post-marketing surveillance.

Quality assurance and data integrity represent fundamental operational pillars at BioNova. Our quality and compliance team, working alongside Clytemnestra, implements standard operating procedures ensuring adherence to Good Clinical Practice guidelines and institutional review board requirements. Advanced technological platforms including ClinicalStream and TrialManager enable real-time monitoring of protocol compliance, adverse event reporting, and data accuracy across active trials. Regular audits by internal and external reviewers maintain the highest standards of scientific and ethical conduct.

Strategic partnerships with leading clinical research organizations and academic medical centers enable efficient execution of global trial networks. Our experienced investigators excel at enrolling diverse patient populations and managing complex protocols across multiple geographic regions. These collaborative relationships facilitate patient recruitment, site activation, and consistent data collection while maintaining rigorous quality standards. Notable projects including MolecularForge demonstrate our commitment to innovative therapeutic development.

Our regulatory strategy team works proactively with health authorities including the FDA and EMA throughout development, leveraging pre-submission meetings to facilitate dialogue regarding trial designs and endpoints. Current pipeline candidates such as Oncora and CardioGuard progress through our development pipeline under the Oncora project, with expertise in breakthrough therapy designations and accelerated approval pathways. Patient safety monitoring constitutes an essential responsibility throughout all clinical programs, ensuring protection and informed consent while advancing therapeutic innovation.

BioNova Global Headquarters operates a comprehensive clinical development division dedicated to advancing novel therapeutic agents through rigorous scientific investigation and regulatory compliance. Our clinical programs span multiple therapeutic areas, including oncology, immunology, cardiovascular disease, and rare genetic disorders. Each program is designed to generate robust efficacy and safety data that support regulatory submissions and ultimately benefit patient populations worldwide. Within our oncology portfolio, Oncora represents a cornerstone investigational agent, while our cardiovascular division actively develops CardioGuard to address unmet clinical needs in cardiac disease management.

The clinical development process at BioNova Global Headquarters follows a systematic four-phase approach aligned with FDA guidance and international regulatory standards. Phase I trials establish safety profiles, dosage ranges, and pharmacokinetic properties in small healthy volunteer or patient populations. Phase II studies evaluate preliminary efficacy and further characterize safety in target patient populations, typically enrolling 100 to 300 participants. Phase III trials confirm efficacy, monitor adverse reactions, and compare investigational products to standard treatments or placebo in large randomized controlled populations. Phase IV studies continue safety surveillance following marketing approval. Quality assurance and data integrity represent fundamental pillars of clinical operations, supported by advanced technological infrastructure including ClinicalStream and TrialManager, which enable real-time monitoring systems that track protocol compliance, adverse event reporting, and data accuracy across all active trials.

BioNova Global Headquarters maintains strategic partnerships with leading clinical research organizations, academic medical centers, and contract research entities to execute global trial networks. Our investigators are experienced in enrolling diverse patient populations and managing complex protocols across multiple geographic regions. These collaborative relationships enable efficient patient recruitment, site activation, and data collection while maintaining consistent quality standards and scientific rigor. The regulatory strategy team works proactively with health authorities including the FDA, EMA, and other national regulatory agencies throughout development, with pre-submission meetings facilitating dialogue regarding trial designs, endpoints, and data requirements.

Critical infrastructure initiatives such as BioNova Trial Management System and BioNova Polaris exemplify our commitment to operational excellence and innovation. These initiatives, overseen by clinical leadership including Leda and Thalia, ensure seamless integration of data management, regulatory compliance, and patient safety monitoring throughout all clinical programs.

enrich_article_data-ai#72f9f8f3:
The pharmaceutical industry has undergone significant transformation through the integration of data and artificial intelligence technologies, fundamentally changing how organizations approach drug discovery, development, and commercialization. BioNova Global Headquarters recognizes that leveraging advanced analytics and machine learning capabilities has become essential for maintaining competitive advantage in modern drug development pipelines. The convergence of big data infrastructure with AI algorithms enables pharmaceutical companies to process vast datasets from clinical trials, genomic research, and real-world evidence more efficiently than traditional methods. This integration has proven particularly valuable in developing innovative therapeutics, exemplified by initiatives such as MolecularForge, which harnesses computational power to accelerate molecular design and validation processes.

Machine learning applications in pharmaceutical development address critical challenges across multiple therapeutic areas through sophisticated computational frameworks. The MLPipeline platform enables BioNova Global Headquarters to utilize predictive models that identify promising drug candidates during the early discovery phase, significantly reducing the time and capital required to advance compounds through preclinical screening. These algorithms analyze molecular properties, protein interactions, and historical efficacy data to predict which molecules are most likely to demonstrate desired pharmacological effects while minimizing adverse side effects. Under the guidance of key researchers including Deucalion, such technological implementations have accelerated the development pathways for candidate therapeutics.

Artificial intelligence has revolutionized the drug development landscape, allowing companies like BioNova Global Headquarters to discover new therapeutic applications for existing compounds and optimize formulations. The sophisticated FeatureStore infrastructure supports comprehensive data integration and analysis, enabling researchers to identify unexpected connections between known drugs and previously untreated conditions. This approach has proven particularly valuable in advancing therapeutic candidates such as Oncora and its specialized formulation Oncora SC, accelerating time-to-market for new indications while leveraging established safety profiles.

Clinical trial optimization and real-world evidence analysis have become increasingly important for demonstrating long-term drug safety and efficacy beyond controlled trial environments. BioNova Global Headquarters integrates electronic health records, insurance claims data, and patient registries using advanced analytics platforms, with patient engagement facilitated through the BioNova Patient Portal. Under the strategic oversight of Nemesis, these insights support post-market surveillance activities and inform healthcare providers about drug performance in clinical practice settings, ultimately enhancing patient outcomes and operational excellence across the pharmaceutical value chain.

enrich_article_drug-discovery#0d82f784:
Drug discovery at BioNova Global Headquarters represents a sophisticated integration of computational biology, high-throughput screening, and medicinal chemistry, exemplified through our development of innovative therapeutics including Oncora and its specialized formulation Oncora SC. Our discovery pipeline begins with comprehensive target identification, where research teams analyze disease pathways and validate potential therapeutic targets through both in vitro and in vivo models. This foundational phase requires extensive literature review, patent landscape analysis, and collaboration with academic institutions to ensure scientific novelty and commercial viability. BioNova Global Headquarters maintains proprietary databases containing over 50 million chemical compounds and genetic targets, enabling rapid assessment of druggability scores and off-target binding potential through systematic evaluation protocols.

The lead identification phase leverages advanced screening platforms powered by MolecularForge, our proprietary computational chemistry platform that integrates artificial intelligence and machine learning algorithms to predict compound behavior and optimize hit selection. High-throughput screening campaigns evaluate thousands of compounds daily against validated targets, utilizing fluorescence-based assays, surface plasmon resonance, and electrophysiology platforms. These technological innovations have reduced our average discovery-to-development timeline by approximately 18 months compared to industry standards, demonstrating the substantial impact of computational integration on research velocity and efficiency.

Lead optimization constitutes the critical bridge between discovery and development phases, where medicinal chemists iteratively modify chemical scaffolds to enhance potency, selectivity, and pharmacokinetic properties. Structure-activity relationship studies inform systematic modifications, while parallel synthesis enables rapid generation of compound libraries. Our optimization teams, guided by leadership including Orpheus and Ares, balance multiple parameters including enzyme inhibition, cellular potency, metabolic stability, and cytochrome P450 interaction profiles to identify candidates with optimal therapeutic potential.

Preclinical characterization and regulatory strategy development occur concurrently with ongoing research initiatives. BioNova collaborates with contract research organizations to conduct comprehensive toxicology studies, safety pharmacology assessments, and pharmacokinetic evaluations. Our in-house bioanalytical laboratory measures drug absorption, distribution, metabolism, and excretion characteristics critical for human dosage projection. Institutional infrastructure improvements, including SOC2 Compliance Remediation and DataLake v2, ensure secure data management and regulatory alignment throughout the discovery process. Regulatory affairs specialists engage with global health authorities, including the FDA and EMA, to establish development pathways aligned with current guidance documents.
